title: pterocarpus pedatus
intro: Pterocarpus pedatus, also known as the African bloodwood tree, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Pterocarpus pedatus include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids in its bark and leaves. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating fever, malaria, and skin infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Pterocarpus pedatus include a decoction of the bark and leaves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and a tincture of the bark, which is used to treat fever and other infections.
